original_abstract: This paper presents the design and implementation of Stanford CoreNLP, an integrated suite of natural language processing tools organized as an extensible pipeline. By providing a broad range of core analysis components—spanning from basic tokenization and POS tagging to full syntactic parsing and coreference resolution—the toolkit has become a foundational resource for the NLP research community, as well as for commercial and government sectors. We attribute the widespread adoption of Stanford CoreNLP to its focused, approachable design: it offers straightforward interfaces and robust, high-quality linguistic models without requiring the significant overhead or architectural "baggage" often associated with larger framework ecosystems. In this work, we detail the system's underlying architecture, its ease of use across various programming environments, and the specific annotators that enable sophisticated text analysis.
humanized_abstract: This paper describes a new release of Stanford CoreNLP, a Java annotated library for natural language processing (NLP) applications that provides a highly flexible analysis pipeline with a wide range of applications, from basic tokenization, part-of-speech tagging and naming entity recognition to fully specified parsing and coreference. We describe its underlying architecture, ease of use for developers, and individual annotators. We argue that the success of Stanford CoreNLP stems from focusing on a small set of applications with a simple, easy-to-use API and high-quality models for linguistics, without the overhead and structural burdens of a full development environment.
